question:
Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..3, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).

C = [[0, 1.5, 0.5], [1.5, -2, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5, -3]]
A_1 = [[-2, 0.5, 1.5], [0.5, -2, -1], [1.5, -1, 2]]
A_2 = [[0, -2, 0.5], [-2, 1, 0], [0.5, 0, 1]]
A_3 = [[-2, -2, 2], [-2, -1, 1.5], [2, 1.5, 2]]
b_1 = -12.0, b_2 = 35.0, b_3 = 12.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?

Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..3, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).

C = [[0, 0.5, 1.5], [0.5, 3, 2.5], [1.5, 2.5, -3]]
A_1 = [[-3, -2.5, -1], [-2.5, 3, -1.5], [-1, -1.5, 2]]
A_2 = [[-3, -3, -2], [-3, 1, 2], [-2, 2, 0]]
A_3 = [[2, -1, 1.5], [-1, 3, 1], [1.5, 1, -3]]
b_1 = 13.0, b_2 = 2.0, b_3 = -4.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?

Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..2, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).

C = [[3, 0.5, 1.5], [0.5, 2, 2], [1.5, 2, -1]]
A_1 = [[2, -1, -0.5], [-1, -2, -0.5], [-0.5, -0.5, -3]]
A_2 = [[3, -2, -1.5], [-2, -1, 1], [-1.5, 1, 1]]
b_1 = -28.0, b_2 = 0.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?
